items.speaker: Ludens
items.text: Such incredible strength, right from your first match! Inviting you was definitely the right call!
Ahem.
That was an absolutely brilliant duel.

items.speaker: Ludens
items.text: You are... Furius himself?! In the flesh?!

items.speaker: Ludens
items.text: Wh-what are you doing here?! Oh, I can't believe I'm seeing you in person! The legendary cre—

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: —Legendary customer service rep who set the record for the fastest response time!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Ahem. Please, allow me to introduce myself properly. I am Furius, and I serve in the finest capacity at Peaks of Prestige's... customer service.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: I had the pleasure of witnessing your duel, and even as a newcomer, your command of strategy was truly remarkable.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Honestly, it's no exaggeration. You could rival even our top players. A rising star of the game!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: You might even become the first ever Apex Duelist.

items.speaker: Ludens
items.text: Exactly, it's the highest title a duelist can achieve!

items.speaker: Ludens
items.text: To become an Apex Duelist, you must defeat all six Lords of Prestige and collect their Medals.

items.speaker: Ludens
items.text: The final Lord, known as the Conqueror of Prestige, remains undefeated to this day. No one has ever qualified to challenge him, and his identity is still a complete mystery...

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: That's precisely correct.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: But if you're willing to take on this challenge, I believe you can collect all six Medals and rise as the first Apex Duelist in the game's history.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: By then, we could use your story to hype the game, buy media, and make Peaks of Prestige the next nationwide sensation like the Agon of Champions! Then... we'd make a killing...

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: N-nothing! You must have misheard me.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Ahem, the important thing is, I truly recommend you set your sights on becoming the Apex Duelist.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: And more importantly, I'll make you a promise.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: The journey there will be filled with thrills and excitement!

options.text: I'll aim to become an Apex Duelist!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Fantastic!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: The first Lord of Prestige isn't far from here. It's the perfect place for a beginner to start climbing the ranks.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: I'll be around for a while. If you want to trade in your Prestige Points for rewards, just come find me.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Well then, enjoy your journey, duelist!

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Hahaha! Splendid, absolutely splendid!

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Masterful strategy! Flawless execution! The way you fought, like a hammer striking metal, fierce and unstoppable!

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Here, Challenger. Take the first Medal of Prestige, the Ironheart Medal.

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Putini, did you catch all that? Learned a thing or two?

items.speaker: Putini
items.text: I think I understand, Master. Life is like a battle, right? You need to see the big picture, know when to strike and when to pull back... to forge your own fate!

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Wh-what?

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: No, no, no! I meant you could stand to learn a thing or two from this fine challenger about playing Peaks of Prestige!

options.text: He's just eager to make progress.

items.speaker: Putini
items.text: That's right! You promised, Master! If you lost, you'd teach me whatever I want today!

items.speaker: Putini
items.text: Please, teach me forging! Now!

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Fine, fine... After dueling a beast like that, I reckon today wasn't wasted.

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Challenger, the next Lord of Prestige awaits you on the other end of the city.

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: With your skill, you just might climb all the way to Apex Duelist! Hahaha!

items.speaker: Putini
items.text: Master, please, back to the forge—

items.speaker: Agrippa
items.text: Hold on, Putini! I just had a brilliant idea. How 'bout you and I play a round? If you win—

items.speaker: Putini
items.text: ABSOLUTELY NOT!!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Agrippa the Ironheart... Each Lord of Prestige seems to have their own unique title.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Maybe I should come up with one for myself too, just in case.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (I'll call myself—)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Sounds pretty good. I could even design an ultimate move inspired by that!)

items.speaker: Host
items.text: Welcome, ladies and gentlemen, to the ultimate showdown of Peaks of Prestige!

items.speaker: Host
items.text: In this match, we present to you—

items.speaker: Host
items.text: The mysterious final Lord of Prestige, bearer of the title Conquerer of Prestige, and the strongest duelist in Peaks of Prestige—Furius!

items.speaker: Host
items.text: Versus—

items.speaker: Host
items.text: The rising star who stormed through the ranks, bearer of five Medals of Prestige, and the very first challenger to the Conqueror of Prestige—{PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: I've been waiting far too long for this moment.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Come, {Male=Mr.;Female=Ms.} {PlayerName}. Show me what you're made of.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: For me, for the spectators, and for Peaks of Prestige... Let's make this duel one for the ages!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: The sixth Lord of Prestige, Furius the Firebrand, accepts the challenge!

options.text: Aim to be the Apex Duelist!
Your flames flicker like a candle in the wind!
Let Peaks of Prestige bring smiles to all!

items.speaker: Host
items.text: And now, I declare—LET THE SHOWDOWN BEGIN!!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: It was a brilliant duel, {Male=Mr.;Female=Ms.} {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: As the developer of Peaks of Prestige, I thought I knew every twist and turn of this game... Yet you still defeated me even after I gave it everything I had.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: I bet word of the first-ever Apex Duelist should be spreading like wildfire among the players.

options.text: But you don't seem all that excited.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Well...

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Even though hosting the final in Colosseum Olymdos got us a lot of attention, maybe because people still don't fully understand the game... the number of new duelists isn't quite what I'd hoped for.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: (Sighs) Am I really destined to be a failed manager again...?

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Hm? Wait a second.
Furius receives a message. His face shifts from stunned to bright-eyed as he nods and bows.
More messages pour in. After quite a while, things finally settle.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Oh... oh my goodness!!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: {Male=Mr.;Female=Ms.} {PlayerName}, did you hear that?!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Sponsors! Sponsors are calling, saying they want to fund Peaks of Prestige!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: And not just sponsors, even the Septimont authorities reached out! They said they're considering recognizing Peaks of Prestige as an official exhibition sport!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: After watching our duel, they said they saw the potential, and they want to help bring it to all of Septimont!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Honestly, this outcome is beyond my wildest dreams.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Thank you. Truly. Thank you so much, {Male=Mr.;Female=Ms.} {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: Phew... maybe, just maybe, one day, the name Peaks of Prestige will echo across all of Solaris!

items.speaker: Furius
items.text: From now on, let the duels continue to bring you joy. Here's to you, our first ever Apex Duelist!

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: Waaah! I lost! I gave it my all and still lost!

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: C-can I still fix this? Maybe if I hadn't used that move... Yes, I should still have a fighting chance!

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: Challenger, t-that match doesn't count! One more time, pleeeeease?

options.text: An old saying from Huanglong goes, "No regrets once the move is made."

items.speaker: Excited Man
items.text: Oh, don't cry, Liliania! You fought so hard! You're amazing no matter what!

items.speaker: Shy Woman
items.text: Th-that's right... No matter if you win or lose, you're still our brightest star...

items.speaker: Excited Man
items.text: Liliania! Liliania!

items.speaker: Shy Woman
items.text: Liliania! Liliania!

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: Wait... so... you weren't just cheering for me because I always won...?

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: (Sobs uncontrollably)

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: No, no. Now's not the time to cry!

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: Congratulations, Challenger! Today, you've shown me the true face of brilliance.

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: Here's your reward, the second Medal of Prestige, the Splendent Medal.

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: If you're aiming for the Apex Duelist title, the next Lord of Prestige usually hangs out by the Grand Lift of Janus.

items.speaker: Liliania
items.text: Hehe, now that you've defeated me, you can't lose to anyone else, you understand? I'll be waiting for the good news of you beating the Conqueror of Prestige!

items.speaker: Marcia
items.text: Oh, I lost...

items.speaker: Marcia
items.text: Well, at least I know you can take good care of yourself, even without my advice.

items.speaker: Marcia
items.text: As your reward... Here's the third Medal of Prestige, the Lifespring Medal.

items.speaker: Marcia
items.text: The next Lord of Prestige can usually be found near the Village of Respite.

items.speaker: Marcia
items.text: May your journey be free of pain. Safe travels, Challenger.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (She's such a mature, gentle doctor...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (But... it feels like everyone's terrified of her potions.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Good for the body, sure. But the taste... Reminds me of a certain Fisalia friend...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (If she and I ever got into a duel, I wonder if it would turn out like this...)

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: Congrats.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: Good news is, win or lose, I get paid the same either way.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: Bad news is... I'm still eight hundred and seventy thousand short.

options.text: You were eight hundred eighty thousand short before. That's real progress!

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: It's the amount I need to finally quit this job.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: Compared to how much I want out, it's still way too slow.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: My goal is to save up one million Shell Credits. Then I can be free from House Grandino.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: I've been grinding toward that for five years. No breaks. No complaints. Followed every order. Juggled four side jobs despite being under constant surveillance...

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: Anyway. Here's your reward. The Dustbound Medal.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: The next Lord of Prestige is near <te href=850172>Cliffside Harbor</te>. Head there.

items.speaker: Raul
items.text: As for me, I've gotta get back to work. Goodbye.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Work... is truly a heavy topic...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Feels like I've caught that same, exhausted aura from Zani sometimes, too...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Maybe... this is how office workers fight in Peaks of Prestige...)

items.speaker: Anxious Man
items.text: Oh well. Guess I'll continue the search for my wife...

items.speaker: Anxious Man
items.text: Feilian Beringal, where are you... Last time, I blamed you for losing the match for your high cost... Now you refuse to fight for me...

items.speaker: Anxious Man
items.text: But really, who else is like you? High attack, massive HP, an amazing skill set...

items.speaker: Anxious Man
items.text: Without you, my perfect deck falls apart!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Wait... Did he just say the Feilian Beringal is his wife?)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (No way... Well... Maybe...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (He's pouring his whole heart into Peaks of Prestige. Gotta respect that.)

items.speaker: Angry Woman
items.text: Ugh! I can't believe I led my team into total annihilation again...!

items.speaker: Angry Woman
items.text: That means the list of people I need revenge on is now... one, two... twenty-six... seventy-eight...

items.speaker: Angry Woman
items.text: ARGHHH! I've already lost to seventy-eight people in Peaks of Prestige!?

items.speaker: Angry Woman
items.text: Just you wait! Once I defeat the other seventy-seven... your turn will come!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (So... the people she wants revenge on are basically everyone who's beaten her...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Let's hope next time she'll tone down the theatrics.)

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The old man falls silent once again.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Maybe he wasn't thinking about anything at all... Maybe he was just waiting for someone to play Peaks of Prestige with him.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: ...Fine.

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: I... lost? So this is what losing feels like...?

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: Because I lost, I can't stop thinking about it... I see... So that's what they've been feeling all this time.

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: So that's why they keep battling when they hear Peaks of Prestige. It all makes perfect sense now!

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: Thank you for helping me realize all this!

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: Uncle Cromer said I should hand this over to anyone who defeats me. Here, take the Nightmare Medal.

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: You're the first to get it. Now, I've gotta get back to the game!

items.speaker: Gallus
items.text: Uncle Cromer! Aunt Agatha! Grandpa Gaferro! Wait for me!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (No kid can resist the pull of a good game...)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (If Encore gets into this, I bet Aalto's wallet won't stand a chance.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Speaking of which, maybe a certain researcher could invent something even more irresistible than Peaks of Prestige.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (He's a genius scientist, after all. And he might even crack the secret behind these strategies.)

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Bravo! Bravo! What a magnificent duel, {PlayerName}!

options.text: Looks like luck wasn't on your side this time, Captain.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Hah! I bet word of our legendary duel will spread to every corner of Septimont!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: You too, my friend!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I'm a man blessed with endless luck, my friend! Well... perhaps a little short on strategy.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Looks like this marks the end of my Peaks of Prestige adventure.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: I gave it my all but still didn't snag that final prize Furius mentioned. But the rewards I picked up along the way? More than enough to help the Troupe live a bit easier.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Ah, that's life, isn't it? Gotta find joy in what you have!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Well then, I must dash. My charming Troupe awaits. Until next time, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Congratulations, {PlayerName}. This victory is truly yours to claim.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Haha, looks like the legend of the very first Apex Duelist won't end with me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: If you had bested me, you'd be on your way to becoming the next legend.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Haha, but no. That path isn't meant for me. I'm a wandering bard, remember?

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: I don't become the legend. I walk beside it, witness it, weave it into song, and most of all, tell its story.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: And the hero of this story is you, {PlayerName}, the Apex Duelist.

items.speaker: Ciaccona
items.text: Look forward to the song I'll write, a song born from this duel, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You really live up to the name. Well fought, my fellow warrior!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Heh, your strategies... they'd even work in real gladiating. I learned a lot today.

options.text: That's what the Apex Duelist is capable of.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: No need for modesty. After a hard-won victory, you should bask in the applause and cheers. Too much humility only dulls the shine of glory.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I bet this win added another brilliant mark to the honor you carry.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Oh, by the way, I heard there's another duelist who's earned the right to challenge the Apex Duelist. A mysterious one.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't know him, but I did catch a glimpse of him once.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Even from the outline of his admittedly broad back, you can tell he's someone who's used to gambling with his life.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't think he'll be able to topple you, but still, be careful when you face him.

options.text: Furius said he's an old acquaintance of mine.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: May victory smile upon you, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Mm, it never hurts to keep an eye out. Good luck, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: An old acquaintance, huh? You've sure got a colorful circle, my fellow warrior! But once you're both on the battlefield, there's no holding back. After all, there'll be only one winner.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Anyway, good luck out there! I look forward to seeing you back at it again, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: ...Let's stop it here, {PlayerName}. The victory is yours.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: Heh, this is the first time I've faced an opponent worthy of bringing my all to an Echo duel since I left the New Federation...

options.text: There's Peaks of Prestige in the New Federation?

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: No... The games there are not for pleasure. Losing there costs more than just your honor or reputation. It's about... paying a much heavier price.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: Indeed. Honestly, it's just a way for the stronger, more ruthless ones to eliminate the ones they despise.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: After founding the Ghost Hounds, I made sure to forbid such cruel displays.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: I feared if we followed that path, we'd lose our humanity, dragged into a darkness where there's no turning back.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: Still, being able to play it like a game, without those burdens... it's a refreshing experience.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: Heh. Just some old memories surfacing. Thank you for listening, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: Now then, congratulations on your final victory.

items.speaker: Calcharo
items.text: I must be going. May the darkness never reach your eyes, {PlayerName}.
